title: ferrotorch-core: GPU reduce(Max/Min) returns first-row instead of last — surfaced by phase 2.6 (#768)
description:
Phase 2.6 conformance test gpu_reduce found that einops::reduce on CUDA returns the wrong result for EinopsReduction::Max and EinopsReduction::Min when the kept axis comes after the reduced axis in left order.

Repro:
  input shape [3, 2], data = [0.5, 4.0, 1.0, 4.5, 1.5, 5.0] on cuda:0
  reduce(t, "b c -> c", EinopsReduction::Max)
  expected: [1.5, 5.0]
  actual:   [0.5, 4.0]   (= input row 0)

The fast path in einops.rs uses cummax/cummin then narrow(dim=1, last, 1) + squeeze. The result selects row 0 instead of the last running-max row, suggesting one of:
  - GPU cummax returns the input unmodified for [1, N, M] view shapes
  - GPU narrow(dim=1, start=N-1, len=1) selects start=0 instead

Sum/Mean reductions work correctly (they use sum_dim, which is GPU-aware and routes through a different code path). Min superficially looks right because the min of the test data IS row 0.

CPU path returns the correct result. Confirmed by direct repro test (now removed).
